parsed_review.summary: CP00-902 is a descriptor-only RP30 embedded-HRX bridge pack (risk A, 10 units, RP30.P01.M05.S03-RP30.P01.M06.S03; 7 PeopleGraph security-audit + 3 Employee implementation rows). Read-only inspection via Read/Grep/Glob confirms the pack-level claim is fully supported. Implementation (packages/hrx/src/registry.js createHrxCp902PeopleGraphEmployeeBridgeDescriptor and src/validators.js validateHrxCp902PeopleGraphEmployeeBridgeDescriptor/Coverage) generates deep-frozen rows with all runtime/write/real-data/secret flags false and enforces PeopleGraph permission-edge/graph-runtime/audit-write closure plus Employee/User separation (reverse-link only, no user-account conflation). Grep found no fs-write/exec/spawn/fetch/http/process.env/eval in source (purely declarative; test uses read-only readFileSync) and no real PII/secrets — only boundary attestations. Tests in test/model.test.js assert CP902 binding, coverage distribution {security_audit:7,implementation:3}, descriptor boundaries, and contract projection, and trace-pass against the live plan and generated contract. Plan pack (docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json), manifest plan_binding_snapshot, contracts/hrx-people-contract.json (latest_pack CP00-902, latest_projection HrxCp902PeopleGraphEmployeeBridgeDescriptor, validation.valid=true, top-level no_write_attestation all-false), README, and prompt distributions are mutually consistent. Authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required=true; Hermes packet emits_runtime_receipt=false (deterministic review-evidence only, not product runtime). CP178+ evidence hardening satisfied: readiness auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1226-byte valid-JSON stdout, no auth failure/0-byte/malformed/tool-call-shaped output). Provenance: verdict is based solely on read-only source inspection; I did not re-execute the validation ladder (npm test / npm run validate / etc.) — those exit codes are pipeline-claimed and not independently re-run in this read-only review. The manifest honestly reports production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false and defers runtime/enterprise-trust opening to human approval at CP00-903, so Claude remaining a read-only non-final reviewer does not block this CP closeout. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
